messages.content:
The thesis statement of an essay usually appears in the:
None of these choices are correct.
topic sentence of a body paragraph.
introduction.
body of the essay.
conclusion.
introduction.

Using your knowledge of Greek and Latin roots, which of the following words means an uncontrollable
desire or madness for burning things?
congress
hydrolysis
pyromania

Pyromania. 

"Pyro-" comes from the Greek word "pyr" meaning "fire" and "-mania" comes from the Greek word "mania" meaning "madness" or "obsession." Therefore, "pyromania" refers to an uncontrollable desire or madness for burning things.

The main purpose of the article about Sequoia and Kings Canyon National Parks is to inform people about
public cave tours.
False
True

False. 

The main purpose of the article about Sequoia and Kings Canyon National Parks is not to inform people about public cave tours but rather to introduce two beautiful national parks located in California and provide information on the various attractions, recreational opportunities, and visitor services available in the parks.

A bar graph:
allows readers to compare information from different time periods.
displays information by plotting points on a graph and connecting the points with
a line.
indicates a certain amount or percentage of something.

Indicates a certain amount or percentage of something. 

A bar graph is a chart that uses bars to compare different categories of data. The bars can be horizontal or vertical, and their lengths represent the values they represent. The height or length of each bar usually represents a certain amount or percentage of something and allows readers to compare the values easily. Therefore, the correct option is "indicates a certain amount or percentage of something."

Fluent readers do not have to practice independent reading to maintain their fluency.
False
True

False. 

Fluent readers still need to practice independent reading to maintain their fluency. While being a fluent reader means that someone is able to read smoothly, quickly, and accurately, it is still important to continue practicing reading to maintain these skills. Regular reading helps keep vocabulary and comprehension skills sharp, and it also helps to develop overall reading comprehension. Therefore, the statement "Fluent readers do not have to practice independent reading to maintain their fluency" is false.

All of the topic sentences for paragraphs in an essay should:
support the thesis statement of the essay.
contain quotations or other types of evidence from sources.
be the first sentence of each paragraph.

Support the thesis statement of the essay. 

The topic sentence of each paragraph should connect to and support the thesis statement of the essay. It is usually the first sentence of the paragraph and it provides the reader with a clear understanding of what the paragraph will be about. The topic sentence should be concise, clear and relevant to the main argument; it should not be a quote or an evidence from a source as this belongs to the body of the paragraph. Therefore, the correct option is "support the thesis statement of the essay."

Paraphrasing means a writer is summarizing longer passages of a text and putting them into her own
words.
False
True

True. 

Paraphrasing is the process of rewording a text so that the meaning stays the same but the language is different. When paraphrasing, a writer is summarizing longer passages of a text and putting them into her own words. This is often done to simplify or clarify complex language, to make a text more accessible to readers, or to incorporate outside information into an essay or research paper. Therefore, the correct statement is "True".

Which of the following patterns of organization would be most effective when writing an essay about the
life of George Washington?
Main Idea and Supporting Details
Organization by Degree
Comparison and Contrast
Cause and Effect
Chronological or Sequential Order

Chronological or Sequential Order. 

The most effective pattern of organization to use when writing an essay about the life of George Washington is Chronological or Sequential Order. This pattern involves presenting events in the order they occurred in time, from earliest to most recent. This would allow the essay to present Washington's life in a coherent and structured way, while also emphasizing the important developments and contributions made by Washington at different stages of his life. By presenting the life of George Washington in this manner, the reader would be able to follow his progress and understand how he grew and changed in response to the challenges of the world around him. Therefore, the correct option is "Chronological or Sequential Order".

The California English Language Arts Standards require that students read ten million words on their own
by the 10th grade.
True
False

False. 

The California English Language Arts Standards do not require that students read ten million words on their own by the 10th grade. This is a common myth. The California Department of Education (CDE) does suggest that students read a certain number of words per year in order to build vocabulary and reading fluency, but this varies by grade level and is not a specific requirement. Additionally, the CDE recommends that students read a variety of different genres and styles, including literature, informational texts, and digital media. Therefore, the statement "The California English Language Arts Standards require that students read ten million words on their own by the 10th grade" is false.

Which of the following sentences contains an idiom?
If I wasn’t so angry I would storm into that office myself!
Charles was really on top of the world after Marsha agreed to go on a date with
him.
Our day at the beach was so rad that we decided to cruise over there again in the
a.m.
None of the choices are correct.
Preston is unemployed but he likes to refer to himself as a professional job
seeker.

Our day at the beach was so rad that we decided to cruise over there again in the a.m.

The sentence "Our day at the beach was so rad that we decided to cruise over there again in the a.m." contains an idiom. The idiom "so rad" means "very cool" or "awesome." It is an informal slang expression that is commonly used by young people. Therefore, the correct option is "Our day at the beach was so rad that we decided to cruise over there again in the a.m."

Readers can use context clues to determine the meaning of words that they do not recognize.
True
False

True. 

Readers can use context clues to determine the meaning of words that they do not recognize. Context clues are words or phrases that surround an unfamiliar word and provide hints about its meaning. These hints can come from the words that come before or after the unfamiliar word, or from other information in the sentence or paragraph. Using context clues, readers can make educated guesses about what the unfamiliar word means and continue reading with a better understanding of the text. Therefore, the statement "Readers can use context clues to determine the meaning of words that they do not recognize" is true.

Fluent readers practice speed, accuracy, and:
connotation.
appropriate expression.
orthography.

Appropriate expression. 

Fluent readers practice speed, accuracy, and appropriate expression. Appropriate expression is the ability to read with appropriate tone, pacing, and emphasis to convey the author's message effectively. Fluent readers use appropriate expression to bring the text to life, making it more engaging and meaningful for the reader. Therefore, the correct option is "Appropriate expression".

Which of the following statements best describes expository essays?
Most expository essays are required to be at least ten paragraphs in length.
They are meant to inform, explain, or describe something for readers.
Thesis statements in expository essays are always the first sentence of the
introduction.

They are meant to inform, explain, or describe something for readers. 

Expository essays are meant to inform, explain, or describe something to the readers. The purpose of an expository essay is to provide a logical and clear explanation of a particular topic or concept using facts, statistics, examples, and evidence to support the argument. The author of an expository essay seeks to persuade the reader to accept a particular point of view on the topic by presenting a balanced, in-depth analysis of the subject. The essay can be of any length, depending on the scope of the topic, and it should have a clear thesis statement that previews the main points to be discussed in the essay. Therefore, the correct option is "They are meant to inform, explain, or describe something for readers."

A person examining the history of a word is using mnemonics.
True
False

False. 

A person examining the history of a word is not using mnemonics. Mnemonics are memory aids, such as acronyms or rhymes, that help people remember information or concepts. Examining the history of a word involves studying the etymology or origins of a word and its development over time. This is a process of research and analysis that seeks to understand how a word has evolved to its current use and meaning. Therefore, the statement "A person examining the history of a word is using mnemonics" is false.

Evidence from a journal article or an encyclopedia does not need to be evaluated for currency or
objectivity.
True
False

False. 

Evidence from a journal article or an encyclopedia needs to be evaluated for currency and objectivity. Currency refers to how recent the information is and whether it reflects the most up-to-date and accurate knowledge on the topic. Objectivity refers to the impartiality and accuracy of the information, which means that it is free from bias and represents all sides of the issue in a balanced manner. Evaluating the currency and objectivity of sources is crucial to ensure that the information used in research or writing is reliable, trustworthy, and useful. Therefore, the statement "Evidence from a journal article or an encyclopedia does not need to be evaluated for currency or objectivity" is false.

Expository writers produce informational texts.
False
True

True. 

Expository writers produce informational texts. The purpose of expository writing is to inform, explain, or describe something in a clear and concise manner. Writers who produce expository texts use facts, evidence, and examples to support their ideas and provide the reader with a logical and well-organized presentation of the information. Examples of expository writing include news articles, reports, how-to guides, research papers, and essays that provide a detailed explanation of a specific topic. Therefore, the statement "Expository writers produce informational texts" is true.

Words have different connotations but not multiple meanings.
True
False

False. 

Words can have both connotations and multiple meanings. Connotation refers to the emotional or cultural associations that are attached to a word, such as the positive or negative feelings that a word might evoke in different people. Multiple meanings, on the other hand, refer to situations where a word has more than one meaning or definition, such as the word "bank," which can refer to a financial institution or the side of a river. These two concepts are different but they can both affect how a word is understood and used in different contexts. Therefore, the statement "Words have different connotations but not multiple meanings" is false.

Which of the following words has a denotation of something that is pure or untouched?
susceptible
None of the choices are correct.
confluence
pristine
vibrant

Pristine. 

Pristine has a denotation of something that is pure or untouched. The word is commonly used to describe something that is in its original, unspoiled condition, such as a pristine forest or a pristine beach. This denotation is related to the word's Latin roots, which mean "fresh" or "new." Therefore, the correct option is "pristine".

Slang terms are figures of speech.
False
True

True. 

Slang terms are figures of speech. A figure of speech is a word or phrase that is used in a non-literal way to create a rhetorical effect or convey a particular meaning. Slang is a type of informal language that is commonly used in everyday speech, particularly by young people. Slang terms often involve figurative language, such as metaphors, hyperbole, and idiomatic expressions. For example, the slang term "beat" can be used to mean "exhausted," which is not its literal meaning but a figurative one. Therefore, the statement "Slang terms are figures of speech" is true.

Which of the following choices is a figure of speech?
an oxymoron
an affix
prosody

An oxymoron. 

An oxymoron is a figure of speech that combines two contradictory terms in order to create an effect or convey a meaning. The term is derived from the Greek words for "sharp" and "foolish," which reflect the paradoxical nature of the expression. Examples of oxymorons include "jumbo shrimp," "open secret," and "pretty ugly." Affix refers to a type of morpheme that is added to the base or root of a word to form a new word. Prosody refers to the patterns of rhythm and sound in language, such as stress, intonation, and phrasing. Therefore, the correct option is "an oxymoron".

Which of the following choices is not an informational text?
an article in a sports magazine
a fictional play
a pamphlet from a health clinic

A fictional play. 

A fictional play is not an informational text. While a fictional play may contain information about characters, settings, and events, its primary purpose is to entertain and tell a story, rather than to inform or explain something. An article in a sports magazine and a pamphlet from a health clinic are both examples of informational texts because they provide information on specific topics in a clear, objective manner. Therefore, the correct option is "a fictional play".

Which of the following choices is the root of the word initiative?
ati
init
tive

Init. 

Initiative is derived from the Latin word "initium", which means "beginning" or "entrance." The root of the word is "init", which means "start" or "begin." Therefore, the correct option is "init".

A poster explaining safety procedures in the lunchroom of a warehouse is an example of:
an expository text.
technical directions.
a functional workplace document.

A functional workplace document. 

A poster explaining safety procedures in the lunchroom of a warehouse is an example of a functional workplace document. These types of documents are designed to convey essential information and instructions to employees in a clear and easily accessible manner. The poster serves as a visual aid that can be quickly referenced by employees to ensure they are following proper safety protocols. Examples of other functional workplace documents include job descriptions, employee handbooks, and safety manuals. Therefore, the correct option is "a functional workplace document."

Writers should always use MLA guidelines when citing sources in expository and persuasive essays.
False
True

False. 

Writers should not always use MLA guidelines when citing sources in expository and persuasive essays. While MLA (Modern Language Association) guidelines are a commonly used citation style, there are other styles as well, such as APA (American Psychological Association) and Chicago. The choice of citation style depends on the specific requirements of the essay or assignment, as well as the preferences of the instructor or publisher. It is important for writers to follow the specific guidelines provided for each assignment or publication to ensure that their citations are accurate and complete. Therefore, the statement "Writers should always use MLA guidelines when citing sources in expository and persuasive essays" is false.

Which of the following sentences uses the word prominent correctly?
The police tried to prominent the suspect before he could escape.
Elliot wanted to buy a prominent so he asked his boss for a raise.
The most prominent feature on Carly’s face is definitely her nose.

The most prominent feature on Carly’s face is definitely her nose. 

"Prominent" means important, famous, or easily noticeable and the only sentence that uses this word correctly is the most prominent feature on Carly’s face is definitely her nose. This sentence correctly uses "prominent" to describe a feature that is noticeable or significant, which in this case, is Carly's nose. The other two sentences have used it inappropriately. In this way, the correct option is "The most prominent feature on Carly’s face is definitely her nose".

Which of the following questions should readers ask themselves when reading an informational text?
What is the main idea or concept being communicated?
How is the main idea developed throughout the text?
How is the information organized and why is it done this way?
Does the author present enough evidence or examples to support his main points
and assertions?
All of the choices are correct.

All of the choices are correct. 

When reading an informational text, readers should ask themselves a variety of questions to understand and analyze the content of the text. These questions can include asking about the main idea or concept being communicated, how the main idea is developed throughout the text, how the information is organized and why it is done this way, and whether the author presents enough evidence or examples to support their main points and assertions. By asking a variety of questions, readers can gain a deeper understanding of the text and form their own opinions and conclusions based on the information presented. Therefore, the correct option is "All of the choices are correct."

Ellipses are used to:
introduce a list or items in a series.
show where text has been left out of a quotation.
divide a sentence or separate ideas from one another.

Show where text has been left out of a quotation. 

Ellipses (…) are used to show where text has been omitted from a quotation. This can be done for a variety of reasons, such as to remove irrelevant information, to shorten a lengthy quotation, or to remove profanity or other inappropriate language. The ellipsis helps to indicate to the reader that some text has been removed while maintaining the integrity of the original quotation. Therefore, the correct option is "show where text has been left out of a quotation."

Which of the following words is a transition that would best be used in a concluding paragraph?
in contrast
as a result
All of the choices are correct.
for example
conversely

As a result. 

"As a result" is a transition that is commonly used in a concluding paragraph to indicate the conclusion or consequence of the main argument or content of the essay. It helps to tie together the main ideas presented in the essay and provide closure to the reader. "In contrast," "for example," and "conversely" are transitions that are more commonly used in the body paragraphs of an essay where the writer is presenting evidence, examples, or counterarguments to support their main argument. Therefore, the correct option is
